geography.data_source: Census 2011 ward boundary shapefiles, New Delhi district. Lutyens zone covering India Gate to Rashtrapati Bhavan corridor.

demographics.data_source: Census 2011 New Delhi district tables. Lowest density zone in Delhi (6042/sqkm) due to government buildings, diplomatic enclaves, and large green spaces (Lodhi Gardens, India Gate lawns).

income_profile.data_source: NSSO 78th Round MPCE distribution (New Delhi district), Central Government Pay Commission 7th CPC salary data. Heavily right-skewed: Lutyens Delhi is India's highest-income urban zone with senior bureaucrats, diplomats, and professional services workforce.

commute_profile.data_source: DMRC Origin-Destination Survey 2022, Census 2011 Table D-13. High car ownership (0.72) reflects affluent demographics. Many government employees have official vehicles. Metro dependency is low (0.28) — used primarily by support staff.

sensitivity_parameters.data_source: Composite scores. Very low fare sensitivity (0.15) due to high income and car dependency. High political mobilisation (0.85) reflects proximity to power centres. High institutional trust reflects government employee demographics. High collective action threshold (0.62) — population is institutionally oriented, uses formal channels not protests.

social_network_parameters.data_source: Estimated from IHDS-II social capital module. Low clustering (0.42) reflects workplace-centric networks rather than residential communities. High cross-income bridging (0.35) reflects mixed-class workplace interactions in government offices.

informal_economy_nodes.name: Khan Market
informal_economy_nodes.data_source: Estimated from NDMC market data. Khan Market is India's most expensive retail destination per sqft (Knight Frank 2023). Low transit dependency (0.22) as most visitors arrive by car.

tier1_agent_archetype_weights.data_source: AGENT_SPEC.md Government Zone template (lines 754-764). Sum = 1.00. Government employees dominate (0.42) reflecting Central Secretariat, ministries, and NDMC offices. Journalist presence (0.02) higher than average due to Lutyens press corps.

tier2_agent_types.data_source: Estimated from central government directory, accredited press list (PIB), and diplomatic enclave data. Senior bureaucrats as Tier 2 agents reflect their outsized policy influence in this zone.
